claim: Every decision shall be personally signed by the human resolver -- today: a decided_by: Stakeholder.id field on the DECIDED Conflict plus git commit authorship.
why: M5, REPLACES split into R-trust-anchor-mechanism + R-trust-anchor-delegation-explicit-only per atomicity discipline (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). Resolver verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «человек обязан сам подписать, если только явно не делегирует это в каждом случае или на кампанию вперед» (English: 'the human is obliged to sign personally, unless he explicitly delegates it, either per-case or for a campaign in advance'). This atom lands the DEFAULT (personal signature) half: the resolver's answer is that the existing decided_by + commit-author pairing IS the anchor; no additional cryptographic layer is required today. In the Go port the mechanism is Conflict.DecidedBy (internal/ontology/conflict.go:48, a Stakeholder.id reference on every DECIDED Conflict) plus git commit authorship (the repo's own author trail). Cadence is 'per decision' (every DECIDED Conflict), not periodic -- the resolver signs each contradiction resolution as it happens, which is stronger than 'quarterly/per-PR' polling. ENFORCED in the Go port by two atomic checks registered directly in the invariant registry (internal/invariants/conflict_decided_held.go): check_decided_has_nonempty_decided_by (asserts DecidedBy is non-empty on every DECIDED Conflict) and check_decided_by_is_known_stakeholder (asserts the DecidedBy value is a registered Stakeholder.id), both exercised by conflict_decided_held_test.go. The historical Python CORRECTION (enforced_by originally cited a thin non-registered delegator wrapping sub-checks in hotam_spec/invariants.py ~line 3415) is resolved in Go: the two atomic checks ARE real ALL registry members, so the bijection holds without the delegator indirection.
